title: The Dress

jokes.text: Johnny discovering ice cold water after Moira's elaborate bathroom ritual
jokes.setup: Johnny expects hot water after normal shower use
jokes.punchline: Moira's absurdly elaborate 45+ minute bathroom routine has used all the hot water
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific comedy - only Moira would have such a ritualistic approach to bathing, and the escalating detail makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, there's no hot water. I just took a normal shower.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, of course there's no hot water, John. I was in there for 45 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: 45 minutes? What were you doing in there?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: My beauty routine. The double cleanse, the toning mist, the serum, the eye cream, the moisturizer, the neck cream, the face mask, the exfoliant, the essence, the sheet mask, the overnight treatment, and then I had to condition my hair, deep condition my hair, apply my hair oil, and do my full body exfoliation and moisturizing regimen.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's... that's insane, Moira.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, this is what happens when you let yourself go, John. One day you're neglecting your skincare routine, and the next day you're unrecognizable.

jokes.text: I'm currently reading a magazine from July, 1987.
jokes.setup: Moira acknowledging their budgetary constraints
jokes.punchline: Her idea of economizing is reading a 30+ year old magazine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Moira's complete disconnect from normal economizing, delivered with perfect specificity

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I've decided we need to tighten our belts. So I'm reading a magazine from 1987.

jokes.text: So I just signed for this, so does that mean I get to keep it? Also, what is it?
jokes.setup: Alexis receiving a package
jokes.punchline: She signed for something without knowing what it is or if it's hers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's carefree attitude toward responsibility

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, a package for me? I love packages!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, what is this? I don't remember ordering anything.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You know what, it doesn't matter. I'm just going to sign for it.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: There. Signed. Now it's officially mine, even though I have absolutely no idea what it is or if it's actually for me.

jokes.text: Give a warm dobrodosli to my red carpet gown
jokes.setup: Moira introducing her dress
jokes.punchline: She uses an obscure foreign word (Croatian for 'welcome') for a simple introduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira vocabulary pretension - using Croatian when English would suffice

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This is my new dress. I call it 'dobrodošli.'

jokes.text: Family, I'd like to you to meet a dear friend whom I've yet to meet.
jokes.setup: Moira introducing her dress as a friend
jokes.punchline: The logical contradiction of calling someone a 'dear friend' she's never met
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic delivered with Moira's characteristic grandiosity

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This is my dear friend... I've never met her.

jokes.text: I've been told definitively it will happen, and I quote, 'ideally sometime this year.'
jokes.setup: Moira claiming definitive information about the premiere
jokes.punchline: The 'definitive' information is completely vague and non-committal
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction between 'definitively' and the vague quote that follows

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I have definitive information about the premiere.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It will either happen, or it won't.

jokes.text: What designer gave you that kind of discount? / Sadly, it's not custom this time around, Alexis. Mommy had to shop off the rack.
jokes.setup: Alexis assumes the $3,700 dress is heavily discounted
jokes.punchline: Moira treats shopping 'off the rack' like a tragic compromise
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' skewed perspective on money and fashion

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so this dress is $3,700? For this price, it must be like, majorly discounted, right?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, this is off the rack.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Off the rack?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes. Which means it's a tragedy we're all going to have to learn to live with.

jokes.text: Fortunately, I discovered something called a 'promo code.'
jokes.setup: Moira explaining how she got the dress
jokes.punchline: She treats discovering promo codes like a groundbreaking achievement
jokes.explanation: Moira's disconnect from modern shopping realities, treating promo codes as exotic

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I found this dress at an estate sale, and I got it for half price because I discovered a promo code online. She treats it like I've just split the atom.

jokes.text: I'm surprised she made it through customs.
jokes.setup: Talking about the dress's journey
jokes.punchline: Personifying the dress as potentially being stopped by customs
jokes.explanation: Continues the personification of the dress in a believable Moira way

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The dress has quite a journey ahead of it. It could be stopped by customs, questioned about its origins, its intentions...

jokes.text: I once passed off a mini horse and three Guinea pigs as service animals, so anything is possible.
jokes.setup: Responding to Moira's customs comment
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals an absurd past customs scam involving multiple animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - casually revealing an outrageous past exploit with perfect specificity and delivery

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that reminds me. One time I was trying to bring like, five macaws and a llama through customs in Peru, and the guy was like 'you can't do that,' and I was like 'okay, but what if I just told him they were emotional support animals?' And apparently that's not a legal defense when you're also wearing a fur coat made of... well, we don't need to get into that.

jokes.text: TBD on the guest list, Alexis, but I love your enthusiasm.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking about her premiere look
jokes.punchline: Moira politely dismissing her while calling uncertainty 'enthusiasm'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's diplomatic dismissal

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So what do you think of my premiere look?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's very... enthusiastic.

jokes.text: I've been neglecting my wellness journey
jokes.setup: David explaining why he wants to see cherry blossoms
jokes.punchline: Using wellness-speak for what's essentially tourism
jokes.explanation: Good David character moment - his tendency to frame everything in trendy lifestyle terms, even simple activities.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm going to see the cherry blossoms. It's a spiritual journey, really. A pilgrimage to witness nature's ephemeral beauty and reconnect with my inner self.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You're going to look at flowers.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm going to *experience* flowers, Patrick. There's a difference.

jokes.text: I learned the hard way from my last trip to Japan. Showed up, no cherry blossoms, turned right back around, it was such a waste.
jokes.setup: David explaining his cherry blossom planning
jokes.punchline: He literally traveled to Japan and left immediately because of timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - overthinking something beautiful into an anxiety-inducing logistics problem

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I traveled to Japan to see the cherry blossoms in peak bloom. I arrived, assessed the situation, and left immediately because the timing was off.

jokes.text: Wow, that's a lot of pressure to put on cherry blossoms.
jokes.setup: David's intense cherry blossom planning
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry observation about the absurd pressure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan response highlighting David's absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So you're telling me you've been stress-eating macarons for three days because you're worried the cherry blossoms won't be 'pink enough'?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: They need to be a very specific shade of blush. It's not just pink, Stevie.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Right. Because nature is definitely going to consult your mood board.

jokes.text: Well, why else would we be driving to Elm Valley?
jokes.setup: David asks rhetorically why they'd go to Elm Valley
jokes.punchline: Stevie's awkward silence before revealing the real reason
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the revelation that this isn't about cherry blossoms. The repetition and pause creates tension.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why would we go to Elm Valley?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Because you said you wanted to go to Elm Valley.

jokes.text: You had the dirty motel sex with? / Well, when you put it that way it sorta cheapens it
jokes.setup: David describing Stevie's encounter
jokes.punchline: Stevie's mild objection to the crude description
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: She was accosted by a man with very aggressive cologne and a Members Only jacket.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I wouldn't say accosted.

jokes.text: What, am I gonna walk around, and admire the cherry blossoms alone, like some pervert?!
jokes.setup: David realizing he might have to visit the gardens alone
jokes.punchline: He equates solitary nature appreciation with being a pervert
jokes.explanation: Perfect David neurosis - his mind immediately goes to the worst possible interpretation of innocent activity

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not going to the gardens alone. What am I, a pervert?

jokes.text: You know what, I hope for your sake, that they have cherry blossoms in prison.
jokes.setup: David feeling betrayed by Stevie's deception
jokes.punchline: His melodramatic threat implies Stevie will go to prison for this 'abduction'
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to his cherry blossom obsession while showing his dramatic flair

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm calling the police. This is kidnapping. You're going to prison, Stevie. I hope you like orange, because that's going to be your new signature color, and frankly, it does nothing for your complexion.

jokes.text: No, that detail actually slipped my mind, so maybe I'll just take it.
jokes.setup: Ted asks if the coffee is decaf
jokes.punchline: Alexis admits she forgot and tries to take it back
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis moment showing her self-centeredness - she'd rather take the coffee back than admit she messed up a simple gesture.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Is this decaf?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, yes. Totally decaf.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Actually, no. I'm sorry, I completely forgot.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Can I just have that back?

jokes.text: Old Shan out there. / Okay, first of all Alexis, she's not old, we went to vet school together.
jokes.setup: Alexis casually calling Shannon 'old'
jokes.punchline: Ted's defensive response inadvertently reveals they're the same age
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but predictable response

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Shannon's been looking so old lately.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, she's the same age as me, so...

jokes.text: Most people are a fan. So when someone's not, it's usually on them.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining Shannon's attitude
jokes.punchline: Her confident assertion that disliking her is always the other person's fault
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's self-confidence bordering on narcissism

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: She just has this, like, really confident assertion that disliking her is always the other person's fault.

jokes.text: Yes you did, you little smooch monster!
jokes.setup: Ted revealing he hooked up with Shannon
jokes.punchline: Alexis's playful, infantilizing response to the revelation
jokes.explanation: Cute character moment but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you did it! You got the girl! Look at you, little Ted, all grown up and making moves.

jokes.text: Beep, beep! Paging Dr. Casanova J. Heartbreaker.
jokes.setup: Ted's revelation about his dating history
jokes.punchline: Alexis's mock-medical page with dramatic middle initial
jokes.explanation: Playful but not particularly sharp humor

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're telling me you've never actually been in a real relationship? Like, ever?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, there was that time in high school—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Okay, I'm literally going to need to create a medical file for you. 'Theodore... P... Rose.' No, wait—'Theodore... M... Rose.' You need a middle initial that sounds serious. Something that screams 'emotional damage.'

jokes.text: Yeah, it's funny because I actually am a doctor. / So am I... / No, you're not. Just-just Ted.
jokes.setup: Ted pointing out he's actually a doctor
jokes.punchline: Alexis's failed attempt to claim the same status, immediately shut down
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Ted's gentle correction of Alexis

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I'm actually a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I'm a doctor too.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: No, you're not.

jokes.text: I've come up with the perfect excuse for you to buy yourself a brand new hot water heater! / Still waiting for the good news.
jokes.setup: Roland promising good news about an 'excuse' to spend money
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dry response that an expensive purchase isn't good news
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline showing Roland's cluelessness and Johnny's pragmatism

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I got some good news for you. I found an excuse to spend money.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not good news, Roland.

jokes.text: As soon we get uh, 'El Tightwad' here to loosen up the purse strings.
jokes.setup: Roland talking about Johnny's financial reluctance
jokes.punchline: His mock-Spanish nickname 'El Tightwad'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun humor that feels lazy

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: His mock-Spanish nickname 'El Tightwad'

jokes.text: And when dear, did our room become so moist?
jokes.setup: Moira noticing the humidity
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically dramatic and slightly inappropriate word choice
jokes.explanation: Good Moira vocabulary choice that sounds vaguely inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This humidity is absolutely suffocating. I feel like I'm being vaginally consumed by the atmosphere.

jokes.text: Yeah, it might be Johnny's aftershave. I'm-I'm sorry, it is pungent.
jokes.setup: Moira's romantic description of honeysuckle
jokes.punchline: Roland bluntly attributes it to Johnny's strong aftershave, then Johnny apologizes
jokes.explanation: Solid deflation of Moira's romantic moment. Roland's tactlessness and Johnny's embarrassment work well together.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: That's not honeysuckle, that's Johnny's aftershave.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry.

jokes.text: Two hours ago. / And we're sure this is the right hotel? / He's not coming. I'm an idiot.
jokes.setup: David asking when Emir was supposed to arrive
jokes.punchline: The awkward revelation that he's very late and Stevie's self-deprecating conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment but fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: When was Emir supposed to arrive?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: He was supposed to be here three hours ago.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Three hours? Where is he?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I don't know. He's not answering his phone.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, this is awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'm used to people not showing up for me.

jokes.text: He's not coming. I'm an idiot.
jokes.setup: Stevie realizes Emir has stood her up
jokes.punchline: Her blunt self-assessment after being humiliated
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Stevie's self-deprecating honesty is perfectly in character, though the joke itself isn't sophisticated.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, I guess I deserved that. I'm not exactly a catch. I'm difficult, I'm weird, I have the personality of a wet blanket, and I smell like old pennies.

jokes.text: Oh, you'll be getting every round.
jokes.setup: David offers to get the first round of drinks
jokes.punchline: Stevie matter-of-factly states he'll be buying all drinks as penance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery. Her matter-of-fact extraction of payment from David is character-consistent.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'll get the first round of drinks.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You'll be buying all of them. As penance.

jokes.text: Can I get a 'hell yeah' for two hard-working women in the 21st Century?
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to bond with Shannon
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt at feminist solidarity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Alexis's awkward attempt at relatability

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So Shannon, I've been thinking a lot about female empowerment lately.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Like, we need to support each other as women, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Shannon
dialogue.line: That's... really great, Alexis.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, so I was thinking we could like, burn our bras together or something?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, not literally burn them. These are all designer.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Maybe we could just like, donate them to a cause? Or take a photo pretending we're burning them?

jokes.text: Mmm! Muah! Muah!
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining Ted told her about the hookup
jokes.punchline: Her exaggerated kissing sounds to describe their encounter
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing physical comedy but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So Ted told me about you two, and... *makes exaggerated kissing sounds* ...that's what he said it was like.

jokes.text: Clearing the air about that little trip you two took to pound town.
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to be casual about Ted and Shannon's encounter
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriately crude euphemism in a professional setting
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment but the humor is somewhat cheap

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So, uh, Ted and Shannon just... you know, got together last night.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, he really... put his deposit in her account, if you know what I mean.

jokes.text: Okay, let's remember this is still a place of work.
jokes.setup: Alexis's inappropriate workplace conversation
jokes.punchline: Ted's understated professional reminder
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response but not particularly memorable

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Alexis, I think we should keep our conversations workplace appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, Ted, it was just a comment about my weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yes, well, the details about your hot stone massage and the 'very flexible' massage therapist are probably better suited for lunch conversation.

jokes.text: Okay, let's remember this is still a place of work.
jokes.setup: Alexis using crude language about his sex life in his office
jokes.punchline: Ted's gentle but firm reminder about workplace appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Good straight man response to Alexis's inappropriate workplace behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, he was really going at it, you know? Like, just completely—

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Alexis, I think we should probably keep this conversation out of the office.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, Ted, it's not that bad.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: It's just... there are professional boundaries we need to maintain here.

jokes.text: LOL.
jokes.setup: Johnny kicking the water heater in frustration
jokes.punchline: Roland saying 'LOL' out loud instead of laughing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland moment - completely missing the point of internet abbreviations

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: LOL

jokes.text: Okay, I'm trying to follow the video here, it says it's for beginners.
jokes.setup: Johnny struggling with what should be a simple repair
jokes.punchline: His frustration that even the 'beginner' video is too complex
jokes.explanation: Relatable frustration humor - Johnny's incompetence with DIY projects despite his business background is good character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm watching a beginner's woodworking video and the man is moving far too quickly. I cannot keep up.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: He's already moved on to the next step and I'm still trying to understand what a 'miter' is.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: This is a beginner's video, David. If I cannot understand a beginner's video, what does that make me?

jokes.text: Well, not all of us can drive around in an 8 cylinder car. / It's a '78 Lincoln, and it's all we could afford! / Right, '78. Vintage.
jokes.setup: Roland criticizing Johnny's 'extravagant' car
jokes.punchline: Roland misinterpreting Johnny's defensive response as bragging about vintage status
jokes.explanation: Good miscommunication humor showing Roland's tone-deafness

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: That's quite the vehicle you got there, Johnny. Must've cost you a pretty penny.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, it's a classic. They don't make them like this anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, so you're bragging about how old it is now? That's what we're doing?

jokes.text: Wow, for someone who can barely stand, she sounds good.
jokes.setup: David watching a drunk woman sing karaoke
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment about her singing despite extreme intoxication
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor - David's ability to find quality in unexpected places while being slightly mean is character-consistent.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, that was... surprisingly competent for someone who appears to be actively dying.

jokes.text: The student driver has my phone, and I have his. We didn't give them back to each other after we exchanged info.
jokes.setup: Emir explaining why he couldn't call
jokes.punchline: The absurd mistake of keeping each other's phones after the accident
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic that's believable but ridiculous

dialogue.speaker: Emir
dialogue.line: I couldn't call you because after the accident, we somehow ended up with each other's phones.

dialogue.speaker: Emir
dialogue.line: I've been trying to figure out why my phone kept getting calls from people asking for someone named David all day.

jokes.text: Um, long story short, I took a pottery class with this one. / Oh, uh, I'm Emir. / Oh, Dane... a. Dana. / Dana?
jokes.setup: David awkwardly explaining his presence
jokes.punchline: His panicked improvisation of a fake name, stumbling over it
jokes.explanation: Perfect David panic response with believable stumbling over the fake name

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm... uh... David... Rosé. No, wait. Rosé David. I mean— my name is David Rosé-David. It's a hyphenated thing.

jokes.text: I'm sorry dear, is your mouth moving? Could you uh, turn off the dehumidifier for a second? Because I can't possibly hear you over the sound of the dehumidifier!
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to talk to Moira
jokes.punchline: Her dramatically overwrought complaint about the dehumidifier's volume
jokes.explanation: Good escalating complaint that's very Moira in its drama

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, can we talk for a second?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: If this is about the dehumidifier, I have already lodged a formal complaint with the manufacturer, the Better Business Bureau, and God.

jokes.text: This gown has exceeded all expectations.
jokes.setup: Moira discussing the expensive dress while Johnny talks about money problems
jokes.punchline: Her complete disconnect from the financial reality Johnny is explaining
jokes.explanation: Good character writing - Moira's obliviousness to Johnny's stress about money while praising the expensive dress is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, we need to talk about our finances. We're hemorrhaging money.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I found this divine Valentino at an estate sale. Only twelve thousand dollars.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Twelve thousand— Moira, did you hear what I just said? We're going broke.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, darling, which is exactly why I need something to lift my spirits. It's a investment in my mental health.

jokes.text: Not with the price of ink today.
jokes.setup: Moira mentioning Alexis's home office expansion
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific concern about ink prices as a major expense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - fixating on an oddly specific detail as if it's a major economic concern

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, if Alexis is expanding her home office, she needs to be aware that ink cartridges are surprisingly expensive. I once spent $847 on printer ink in a single month.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, that's insane. I don't even print anything.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that was before I realized I could just not print things. Game changer.

jokes.text: This dress is for the family. To remind us that our futures lie outside this town. It feels these days like I'm the only one who hasn't veered off course.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting the expensive dress isn't practical
jokes.punchline: Moira's grandiose justification that her extravagant purchase is actually for the family's benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira rationalization - turning selfish spending into family martyrdom

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, Johnny, this dress is an investment in the family's morale. When I look this fabulous, everyone's mood elevates by at least forty percent. I'm essentially a mood ring, except I'm a dress, and I cost significantly more.

jokes.text: Yeah, I don't think that's relevant. / Ahem. 'Dear Melanie.' / Melanie? Is this the girl from the falafel place?
jokes.setup: Ted claiming something isn't relevant while clearly about to read it
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately identifying the person from minimal information
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ted's transparency and Alexis's knowledge of his social life

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: This isn't really relevant, but I did get an email from someone named David...

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's my brother.

jokes.text: fal-awful saying this over text
jokes.setup: Ted reading his breakup text aloud
jokes.punchline: His accidental mispronunciation creating 'fal-awful' instead of 'feel awful'
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay accident - Ted's stumble creates a better word than intended. The accidental nature makes it feel authentic.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I wanted to reach out and say that I fal-awful about how things ended between us.

jokes.text: I really... fal-awful saying this over text
jokes.setup: Ted reading his own text aloud
jokes.punchline: His 'fal-awful' pun combining 'falafel' and 'awful'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Ted's character, though not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, I tried the falafel at that new place, and it was absolutely fal-awful.

jokes.text: 'Cool!' Exclamation mark. / And it looks like you ended up walking her dog for her? / Well, she seemed really upset.
jokes.setup: Reading Melanie's casual response to being dumped
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Ted still did favors for her after rejecting her
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ted's people-pleasing nature

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: She just texted me back 'ok cool.' That's it. 'Ok cool.'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wow. That's... really casual.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I know! And then she asked me to notarize some documents for her business.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, after she dumped you?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And you did it?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, yes.

jokes.text: And that cute lil' butt.
jokes.setup: Alexis giving a heartfelt speech about wanting Ted
jokes.punchline: She ends the romantic moment with a crude physical compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis character moment - her inability to maintain romantic sincerity without adding something crude is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I just... I think you're really special, Ted. You make me feel things I've never felt before. You're kind and thoughtful and you actually care about me. I don't think I've ever felt this way about anyone, and I just wanted you to know that you mean so much to me, and—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Also, you have a really great ass.

jokes.text: And that cute lil' butt.
jokes.setup: Alexis's heartfelt declaration about wanting Ted
jokes.punchline: She ends the sincere moment with a playful physical compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character beat that undercuts the sincerity in an Alexis way

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I want Ted. I want to be with him. I want to wake up next to him every morning for the rest of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Also, his calves are insane.

jokes.text: Oh no, I was, but then Tammy kept buying shots, and the whole room got behind me in like, a really big way.
jokes.setup: David explaining why he's still at karaoke
jokes.punchline: His pride in winning over the karaoke crowd
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's unexpected social success

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm staying. I've spent the last twenty minutes building a rapport with this crowd, and I'm not going to throw that away just because you want to leave.

jokes.text: Oh my God! Hello! Um, hi! Can I get another drink then, to the room, please? Thanks! / I actually just meant like, your mini-bar.
jokes.setup: Emir offering to cover David's room charges
jokes.punchline: David's immediate attempt to exploit the generosity, followed by Emir's clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect David opportunism immediately followed by reality check

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, well in that case, could you also cover my minibar, my spa services, my dry cleaning, and any incidental charges?

dialogue.speaker: Emir
dialogue.line: No, no. Just the room.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Just the room.

jokes.text: I actually just meant like, your mini-bar.
jokes.setup: David ordering expensive drinks thinking everything is covered
jokes.punchline: Emir clarifying he only meant the mini-bar, not full room service
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of David's excitement - the clarification about limits creates a nice reversal of his expectations.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So everything is covered?

dialogue.speaker: Emir
dialogue.line: The mini-bar, yes.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, I thought you meant—

dialogue.speaker: Emir
dialogue.line: Just the mini-bar. Not the full room service.

jokes.text: Oh no, I finished that.
jokes.setup: Emir clarifying he meant the mini-bar
jokes.punchline: David's casual admission he already consumed the entire mini-bar
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing David's thorough approach to free drinks

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, that's going to be a problem because I may have already consumed the entire mini-bar.

jokes.text: Can you stop yelling, please?! I had 14 polar bear shots last night.
jokes.setup: David complaining about noise
jokes.punchline: The specific number of shots he consumed
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his karaoke night with specific detail that makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Do you have any idea what time it is? I've been listening to you people stomp around like a herd of wildebeest for the past three hours. I have a headache, I'm exhausted, and I've had exactly four glasses of wine.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Four.

jokes.text: So before I send her back, take a mental picture. And perhaps an actual picture as well?
jokes.setup: Moira deciding to return the dress
jokes.punchline: Her request for both mental and physical documentation of the moment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's need to memorialize everything

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'd like to return this dress. And I'm going to need both a mental and a physical documentation of this moment, because it may never happen again.

jokes.text: And those are the shoes we're going with? / What do you think? / No.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking about Moira's shoe choice
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate, blunt rejection
jokes.explanation: Good economy and timing but not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, why are you wearing those shoes?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Because I like them.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: But they're so...

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Ugly? Yes. That's the point.

tmdb_overview: Johnny finds himself under financial pressure with the arrival of Moira’s high-priced red carpet gown for the Crows movie premiere. David is conned into taking a road trip with Stevie.
insight: Character comedy drives a 76-point episode on modest density and understated emotional payoffs.
